edu001.title: Online vs Traditional Education
edu001.topic: Some people believe that online learning is more effective than traditional classroom education.
edu001.instruction: Do you agree or disagree with this statement? Give reasons and examples to support your answer.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the topic and clearly state your position (agree, disagree, or partially agree)
Body 1: Present the first main reason supporting your view with specific examples (e.g., flexibility, accessibility for online; interaction, discipline for traditional)
Body 2: Present the second main reason supporting your view
Body 3 (Optional): Acknowledge the opposing view but explain why your view is stronger
Conclusion: Restate your position and summarize main points

edu002.title: Academic Knowledge vs Practical Skills
edu002.topic: Some people think that university education should focus mainly on academic knowledge, while others believe it should concentrate more on practical skills.
edu002.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate and state that both sides will be discussed, followed by your opinion
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of focusing on academic knowledge (e.g., foundation for research, critical thinking)
Body 2: Discuss the benefits of focusing on practical skills (e.g., employability, readiness for the workforce)
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., a balanced approach is best) and explain why
Conclusion: Summarize the discussion and reiterate your final thought

edu003.title: Academic Stress Among Students
edu003.topic: Many students nowadays feel stressed and overwhelmed by academic pressure.
edu003.instruction: What are the causes of this problem, and what solutions can schools and teachers implement to reduce student stress?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the problem of academic stress among students
Body 1: Discuss the main causes of this stress (e.g., heavy workload, parental expectations, peer pressure)
Body 2: Propose practical solutions for schools and teachers (e.g., adjusting curriculum, providing psychological counseling, promoting extracurricular activities)
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions, and emphasize the importance of addressing the issue

edu004.title: Role of Teachers: Facilitators vs Lecturers
edu004.topic: Teachers should act more as facilitators of learning rather than traditional lecturers.
edu004.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the topic and state the extent of your agreement or disagreement
Body 1: Discuss the advantages of teachers acting as facilitators (e.g., encouraging independent thinking, active learning)
Body 2: Acknowledge the importance of the traditional lecturer role in certain contexts (e.g., explaining complex theories, providing structure)
Conclusion: Summarize your arguments and reaffirm your stance

edu005.title: University Accommodation: Campus vs Home
edu005.topic: Some people argue that university students should live on campus, while others believe living at home is better.
edu005.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the two differing views on student accommodation and state your opinion
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of living on campus (e.g., independence, social networking, proximity to university facilities)
Body 2: Discuss the benefits of living at home (e.g., saving money, family support, comfort)
Body 3: Provide your own opinion with supporting reasons
Conclusion: Bring the essay to a close by summarizing the main points

edu006.title: Technology Distraction in Classrooms
edu006.topic: The increasing use of technology in classrooms can sometimes distract students from learning.
edu006.instruction: What problems can this cause, and how can schools address them effectively?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State the phenomenon of technology distraction in classrooms
Body 1: Analyze the specific problems caused (e.g., short attention span, cyberbullying, academic dishonesty)
Body 2: Suggest effective solutions for schools (e.g., strict device policies, incorporating tech into engaging lessons, digital literacy training)
Conclusion: Summarize the points and highlight the need for a balanced approach to tech in education

edu007.title: Free University Education
edu007.topic: University education should be free for all students.
edu007.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Provide reasons and examples.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the topic and clearly state whether you agree or disagree
Body 1: Present arguments for free education (e.g., equal opportunities, social mobility, skilled workforce)
Body 2: Present counterarguments or reasons against (e.g., economic burden on the state, potential decrease in education quality)
Conclusion: Reiterate your position based on the arguments discussed

edu008.title: Assessment Methods: Exams vs Continuous Assessment
edu008.topic: Some people believe that exams are the best way to assess students’ abilities, while others think continuous assessment is more effective.
edu008.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present both assessment methods and state your own opinion briefly
Body 1: Discuss the advantages of exams (e.g., standardized, tests performance under pressure, memory retention)
Body 2: Discuss the advantages of continuous assessment (e.g., reduces stress, tracks progress over time, encourages consistent effort)
Body 3: Give your own opinion on which method is superior or suggest a hybrid approach
Conclusion: Summarize the points and provide a final closing statement

edu009.title: Graduate Employment Difficulties
edu009.topic: Many graduates struggle to find suitable employment after finishing university.
edu009.instruction: What are the main reasons for this issue, and what can universities do to better prepare students for the workforce?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the problem of high graduate unemployment or underemployment
Body 1: Identify the main reasons (e.g., gap between academic theory and practical skills, oversupply of graduates, lack of soft skills)
Body 2: Suggest actionable solutions for universities (e.g., mandatory internships, career counseling, partnering with industries)
Conclusion: Summarize the main points and emphasize the role of universities in solving the issue

edu010.title: Life Skills in School Curriculum
edu010.topic: Schools should include more life skills training (such as communication and financial management) in their curriculum.
edu010.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your answer with relevant examples.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and clearly state your position
Body 1: Explain the importance of communication skills (e.g., crucial for teamwork, personal relationships, future careers)
Body 2: Explain the importance of financial management skills (e.g., avoiding debt, independent living, economic stability)
Body 3 (Optional): Address potential drawbacks (e.g., crowded curriculum) and explain why life skills are still worth prioritizing
Conclusion: Restate your thesis and summarize the benefits of teaching life skills

edu011.title: Online Courses vs Traditional Universities
edu011.topic: Some people believe that online courses can completely replace traditional universities in the future, while others think universities will always be necessary.
edu011.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate regarding the future of online courses and traditional universities, and state your opinion.
Body 1: Discuss why some believe online courses can replace universities (e.g., lower costs, accessibility, flexibility for adult learners).
Body 2: Discuss why universities will always be necessary (e.g., hands-on practical labs, campus networking, soft skills development, prestige).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., a hybrid model is the most likely and beneficial future).
Conclusion: Summarize the main points and reiterate your stance.

edu012.title: Lack of Motivation in Online Learning
edu012.topic: Many students lack motivation in online learning environments.
edu012.instruction: What are the main causes of this problem, and what measures can teachers and institutions take to increase student engagement?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State the problem of declining student motivation in online learning environments.
Body 1: Outline the main causes (e.g., lack of face-to-face interaction, isolation, technical distractions, absence of a structured study environment).
Body 2: Suggest measures for teachers and institutions (e.g., utilizing interactive software, organizing virtual group works, regular check-ins and feedback).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions, emphasizing the importance of adapting teaching methods.

edu013.title: Mandatory Volunteer Work for University Students
edu013.topic: University students should be required to participate in volunteer or community service activities as part of their graduation requirements.
edu013.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Provide reasons and examples.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and clearly state your position (agree or disagree).
Body 1: Present arguments supporting mandatory volunteering (e.g., builds empathy, develops real-world soft skills, benefits the local community).
Body 2: Discuss potential drawbacks or opposing views (e.g., adds to academic stress, takes time away from studies or paid work).
Body 3: Explain why the benefits outweigh the drawbacks (if agreeing) or vice versa.
Conclusion: Restate your thesis and summarize the main arguments.

edu014.title: STEM Subjects vs Arts and Humanities
edu014.topic: Some people argue that schools should focus more on science and technology subjects, while others believe that arts and humanities are equally important.
edu014.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate between prioritizing STEM versus Arts/Humanities, and state your opinion.
Body 1: Discuss the importance of STEM subjects (e.g., driving economic growth, technological innovation, high demand for tech jobs).
Body 2: Discuss the value of arts and humanities (e.g., fostering creativity, critical thinking, cultural understanding, empathy).
Body 3: Provide your opinion (e.g., a balanced curriculum is essential for producing well-rounded individuals).
Conclusion: Summarize both perspectives and reaffirm your conclusion.

edu015.title: Academic Dishonesty and Cheating
edu015.topic: Cheating in exams and academic dishonesty are increasing in many educational institutions.
edu015.instruction: What are the causes of this problem, and what solutions can schools implement to prevent it?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the rising issue of academic dishonesty and cheating in schools.
Body 1: Explore the causes (e.g., intense pressure to achieve high grades, easy access to information/AI tools, poor time management).
Body 2: Propose solutions for educational institutions (e.g., redesigning assessments to focus on critical thinking, utilizing plagiarism detection software, implementing strict honor codes).
Conclusion: Summarize the main causes and the most effective solutions to preserve academic integrity.

edu016.title: Adapting Teaching to Individual Learning Styles
edu016.topic: Teachers should adapt their teaching methods to suit individual learning styles.
edu016.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state the extent of your agreement.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of tailored teaching (e.g., maximizes individual potential, keeps students engaged, accommodates visual/auditory/kinesthetic learners).
Body 2: Discuss the practical challenges and limitations (e.g., extremely difficult in large classrooms, requires massive resources and time from teachers).
Body 3 (Optional): Propose a realistic middle ground (e.g., using a variety of methods rather than strictly personalizing for every single student).
Conclusion: Summarize the arguments and restate your nuanced position.

edu017.title: Studying Abroad vs Studying Locally
edu017.topic: Some people think that studying abroad provides better educational opportunities, while others believe that studying in one's home country is more beneficial.
edu017.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State the two opposing views on where to pursue higher education and introduce your opinion.
Body 1: Analyze the benefits of studying abroad (e.g., exposure to new cultures, learning foreign languages, accessing top-tier global universities).
Body 2: Analyze the benefits of studying locally (e.g., lower financial burden, building a strong local professional network, being close to family).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., it depends on the student's career goals and financial situation).
Conclusion: Briefly summarize the main points and reiterate your final thought.

edu018.title: Overcrowded University Classrooms
edu018.topic: Many universities face overcrowded classrooms due to increasing student enrollment.
edu018.instruction: What problems does this cause, and what solutions can universities adopt to improve the situation?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the growing issue of overcrowded classrooms in higher education.
Body 1: Discuss the problems caused by this trend (e.g., lack of individual attention from professors, noisy environments, decreased quality of education, strained facilities).
Body 2: Suggest practical solutions (e.g., hiring more teaching staff, offering hybrid/online course alternatives, expanding campus infrastructure).
Conclusion: Summarize the problems and the necessary steps universities must take.

edu019.title: Eliminating Homework in Schools
edu019.topic: Homework should be eliminated in primary and secondary schools.
edu019.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your answer with examples.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and clearly state your position (e.g., partially agree/disagree).
Body 1: Acknowledge the arguments for eliminating homework (e.g., reduces stress, allows time for extracurricular activities and family).
Body 2: Present the arguments for keeping homework (e.g., reinforces classroom learning, teaches discipline and time management).
Body 3: Explain your balanced view (e.g., homework should be reduced and made more meaningful rather than completely eliminated).
Conclusion: Restate your thesis and summarize the main arguments.

edu020.title: Accuracy of University Rankings
edu020.topic: Some people believe that university rankings are an accurate measure of educational quality, while others argue that rankings do not reflect the true value of an institution.
edu020.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate surrounding the reliability of university ranking systems and state your opinion.
Body 1: Discuss why rankings are considered accurate (e.g., based on objective metrics like research output, funding, and faculty qualifications).
Body 2: Discuss why rankings might be misleading (e.g., they often ignore teaching quality, student well-being, and favor wealthy research institutions).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., rankings are a useful reference but should not be the sole deciding factor for students).
Conclusion: Summarize the discussion and reiterate your final stance.

edu021.title: Stress Among University Students
edu021.topic: Many university students experience high levels of stress during their studies.
edu021.instruction: What are the main causes of this problem, and what can universities do to reduce student stress?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the problem of high stress levels among university students.
Body 1: Discuss the main causes (e.g., academic workload, financial pressures, fear of future unemployment).
Body 2: Propose practical solutions for universities (e.g., offering free mental health counseling, providing financial aid/scholarships, organizing stress-relief activities).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions, emphasizing the university's role in supporting students.

edu022.title: Online vs Traditional Classroom Learning
edu022.topic: Online learning is more effective than traditional classroom learning.
edu022.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Give reasons and examples to support your opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state your position clearly (e.g., disagree or partially agree).
Body 1: Present arguments for traditional learning (e.g., direct interaction with teachers, structured environment, fewer distractions).
Body 2: Present arguments for online learning (e.g., flexibility, lower costs, access to global resources).
Body 3: Explain why one is more effective than the other based on your stance.
Conclusion: Restate your thesis and summarize your main arguments.

edu023.title: Teachers' Focus: Academic vs Moral Values
edu023.topic: Some people believe that teachers should focus mainly on academic performance, while others think they should also teach moral values and life skills.
edu023.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate regarding the primary role of teachers and state your opinion.
Body 1: Discuss why some prioritize academic performance (e.g., passing standardized tests, preparing for university, core job responsibility).
Body 2: Discuss why others emphasize moral values and life skills (e.g., shaping good citizens, preventing bullying, holistic development).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., academic performance is important, but moral guidance is essential for a well-rounded individual).
Conclusion: Summarize both views and reaffirm your stance.

edu024.title: Managing Technology Distractions in Classrooms
edu024.topic: The increasing use of technology in classrooms sometimes distracts students from learning.
edu024.instruction: What problems does this cause, and how can schools ensure technology is used effectively?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State the issue of technology causing distractions in educational settings.
Body 1: Analyze the problems caused (e.g., reduced attention spans, lower academic performance, lack of social interaction).
Body 2: Suggest solutions for schools (e.g., implementing strict device-usage policies, training teachers to integrate tech meaningfully into lessons, blocking non-educational sites).
Conclusion: Summarize the problems and emphasize that technology should be a tool, not a distraction.

edu025.title: Free University Education for All
edu025.topic: University education should be free for all students.
edu025.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the topic and state the extent to which you agree or disagree.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of free education (e.g., equal access regardless of socioeconomic status, highly educated workforce).
Body 2: Discuss the drawbacks (e.g., huge burden on taxpayers, potential overcrowding, devaluation of degrees).
Body 3 (Optional): Propose a compromise (e.g., free education for top-performing students or low-income families).
Conclusion: Restate your position and summarize your main points.

edu026.title: Group Projects vs Individual Assignments
edu026.topic: Some people think that group projects are more beneficial for students, while others believe individual assignments are more effective.
edu026.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate between group work and individual study, and state your opinion.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of group projects (e.g., develops teamwork, communication skills, mimics real-world workplaces).
Body 2: Discuss the benefits of individual assignments (e.g., accurately measures personal understanding, fosters independence, prevents 'free-riding').

Body 2: Arguments for supporting pet shelters (e.g., moral responsibility for domestic animals, community safety, preventing disease spread).

env025.title: Urban Heat Island Effect
env025.topic: Temperatures in cities are often significantly higher than in surrounding rural areas.
env025.instruction: What are the causes of this 'heat island effect', and how can urban planners reduce it?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Define the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect.
Body 1: Discuss the causes (e.g., concrete and asphalt absorbing heat, lack of vegetation, heat from AC and vehicles).
Body 2: Suggest solutions for urban planners (e.g., 'cool roofs', increasing tree canopy/green spaces, promoting wind corridors between buildings).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions.

env026.title: Ban on Single-use Plastics
env026.topic: Governments should completely ban the production and sale of all single-use plastics.
env026.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your level of agreement with a total ban on single-use plastics.
Body 1: Discuss the environmental benefits (e.g., massive reduction in ocean waste, saving marine life, reducing microplastics).
Body 2: Discuss the challenges (e.g., higher costs for small businesses, lack of affordable alternatives for certain products like medical supplies).
Conclusion: Reiterate your position, perhaps suggesting a phased ban.

env027.title: Wind Farms: Clean Energy vs Visual Pollution
env027.topic: Some believe wind farms are essential for green energy, while others argue they are eyesores that damage the landscape.
env027.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the controversy over wind turbine installations.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits (e.g., zero carbon emissions, energy independence, sustainable long-term solution).
Body 2: Discuss the aesthetic and environmental concerns (e.g., visual impact on historic landscapes, noise for residents, danger to birds).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., climate change is a bigger threat than visual changes, but placement should be careful).
Conclusion: Summarize and finalize your stance.

env028.title: Soil Degradation and Food Security
env028.topic: Soil quality is declining globally due to intensive farming and chemical use.
env028.instruction: What problems does this cause, and how can sustainable farming practices help?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the problem of declining soil health.
Body 1: Analyze the problems (e.g., reduced crop yields, desertification, lower nutritional value of food).
Body 2: Discuss how sustainable practices help (e.g., crop rotation, organic fertilizers, no-till farming to preserve soil structure).
Conclusion: Summarize the impact and the necessity of shifting agricultural methods.

env029.title: Living in Harmony with Nature
env029.topic: Modern life has made it impossible for people to live in harmony with nature.
env029.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state your level of agreement.
Body 1: Discuss the disconnect caused by modern life (e.g., urbanization, technology addiction, consumerist habits).
Body 2: Discuss how harmony is still possible (e.g., sustainable living choices, urban greening projects, shifting back to seasonal diets).
Conclusion: Restate your position, perhaps emphasizing that harmony requires conscious effort.

env030.title: Public Funding for Space vs Environment
env030.topic: Some people think that money should be spent on exploring space, while others believe it should be used to solve environmental problems on Earth.
env030.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the debate over funding priorities between space and Earth.
Body 1: Discuss the value of space exploration (e.g., satellite technology for monitoring Earth, potential for future resources, scientific curiosity).
Body 2: Discuss the urgency of Earth's environment (e.g., immediate threat of climate change, pollution, biodiversity loss).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., we must prioritize Earth's habitability first, though some space tech actually helps us protect the environment).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude.

env031.title: Climate Change and Developing Nations
env031.topic: Developing countries are often the most affected by climate change, despite contributing the least to global emissions.
env031.instruction: What problems does this cause, and how can international community support these nations?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State the injustice of climate change impacts on developing nations.
Body 1: Discuss the specific problems (e.g., extreme weather destroying fragile economies, food insecurity, climate refugees, lack of infrastructure to cope).
Body 2: Propose solutions for international support (e.g., climate finance funds, technology transfer for renewable energy, debt relief in exchange for conservation).
Conclusion: Summarize and emphasize global ethical responsibility.

env032.title: Impact of the Fashion Industry
env032.topic: The 'fast fashion' industry is one of the most damaging sectors to the environment.
env032.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree? Support your answer with reasons.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Define fast fashion and state your level of agreement regarding its environmental cost.
Body 1: Discuss the environmental damage (e.g., massive water consumption, chemical dyes polluting rivers, textile waste in landfills).
Body 2: Discuss other factors (e.g., transportation emissions, unethical labor as an indirect social environmental cost).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps calling for 'slow fashion' and recycling.

env033.title: Hydroelectric Power: Clean Energy vs Ecosystem Harm
env033.topic: Some people believe hydroelectric dams are a perfect source of renewable energy, while others argue they destroy river ecosystems.
env033.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the dual nature of hydropower.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits (e.g., stable power source, no direct CO2 emissions, flood control/irrigation).
Body 2: Discuss the environmental drawbacks (e.g., blocking fish migration, flooding large forest areas, displacing local communities).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., it is a valuable tool but requires careful environmental impact assessments).
Conclusion: Summarize and provide a closing thought.

env034.title: Food Waste Crisis
env034.topic: A significant amount of food produced for human consumption is wasted every year.
env034.instruction: What problems does food waste cause, and how can individuals and businesses reduce it?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the paradox of global hunger vs. massive food waste.
Body 1: Analyze the environmental problems (e.g., methane emissions from landfills, wasting the water/land used in production).
Body 2: Suggest solutions for individuals (e.g., meal planning, composting) and businesses (e.g., donating surplus food, improving storage tech).
Conclusion: Summarize and call for a shift in how society values food.

env035.title: Banning Cars in City Centers
env035.topic: To reduce pollution, cars should be completely banned from the centers of large cities.
env035.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your viewpoint.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on the proposal to create car-free city centers.
Body 1: Discuss the environmental and social benefits (e.g., improved air quality, reduced noise, safer streets for pedestrians, promoting walking/cycling).
Body 2: Address the practical challenges (e.g., impact on businesses, accessibility for the elderly/disabled, need for massive public transport investment).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance (e.g., supporting the ban if alternative transport is improved).

env036.title: Individual Diet and the Environment
env036.topic: Some people believe that switching to a vegetarian diet is the best way to help the environment, while others think other lifestyle changes are more effective.
env036.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate on the environmental impact of meat consumption vs. other habits.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of a vegetarian diet (e.g., reducing land use, lowering methane from livestock, saving water).
Body 2: Discuss other lifestyle changes (e.g., reducing flights, switching to green energy, cutting plastic use).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., diet is a powerful individual tool, but systemic changes in energy/transport are also vital).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude on the need for holistic change.

env037.title: Rising Sea Levels
env037.topic: Rising sea levels due to global warming threaten many coastal cities and island nations.
env037.instruction: What are the consequences of this trend, and how can governments prepare?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Identify the threat of rising sea levels.
Body 1: Discuss the consequences (e.g., permanent loss of land, saltwater intrusion into crops, displacement of millions of people).
Body 2: Propose preparation strategies (e.g., building sea walls, restoring mangroves, planning 'managed retreat' for vulnerable populations).
Conclusion: Summarize the impact and the need for long-term planning.

env038.title: The Role of Green Spaces in Cities
env038.topic: Every new building project in cities should be required to include green spaces such as roof gardens or parks.
env038.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state your position.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of urban greenery (e.g., cooling the city, absorbing CO2, improving mental health, increasing biodiversity).
Body 2: Discuss the challenges (e.g., increased construction costs, maintenance requirements, space limitations in dense areas).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, emphasizing the long-term livability of cities.

env039.title: Protecting Natural Landscapes vs Tourism
env039.topic: Some people believe that natural landscapes should be kept untouched, while others argue they should be developed for tourism to support the economy.
env039.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the conflict between preservation and economic development via tourism.
Body 1: Discuss the arguments for preservation (e.g., protecting fragile ecosystems, keeping wilderness for scientific value, moral duty to nature).
Body 2: Discuss the benefits of tourism (e.g., economic growth for local regions, funding for park maintenance).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., only 'low-impact' development should be allowed).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude.

env040.title: Chemical Fertilizers and the Environment
env040.topic: The widespread use of chemical fertilizers in agriculture is causing environmental problems.
env040.instruction: What problems does this cause, and what are the alternatives to chemical fertilizers?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State the environmental concern regarding industrial fertilizers.
Body 1: Analyze the problems (e.g., soil degradation, water runoff leading to algae blooms in rivers, killing beneficial insects).
Body 2: Propose alternatives (e.g., organic compost, manure, crop rotation, nitrogen-fixing plants).
Conclusion: Summarize and stress the need for sustainable farming.

env041.title: International Aid for Environmental Disasters
env041.topic: Wealthy nations have a moral obligation to help poorer nations cope with environmental disasters.
env041.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state your position on the responsibility of wealthy nations.
Body 1: Discuss the moral reasons for aid (e.g., historical emissions of developed countries, global solidarity, humanitarian ethics).
Body 2: Discuss the benefits of aid for global stability (e.g., preventing mass migration, protecting global supply chains).
Body 3: Address potential counterarguments (e.g., focus on domestic issues first) and explain why international aid is still necessary.
Conclusion: Restate your thesis and summarize the main arguments.

env042.title: Natural Gas: Cleaner Bridge or Fossil Fuel Trap?
env042.topic: Some people believe natural gas is a good 'bridge fuel' toward a green future, while others argue we should move directly to renewables.
env042.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present natural gas as a controversial middle ground in the energy transition.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of natural gas (e.g., lower CO2 than coal, reliable base-load power while renewable tech matures).
Body 2: Discuss the arguments for skipping directly to renewables (e.g., methane leaks, continued investment in fossil infrastructure, the urgency of the climate crisis).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., it is a risky distraction that delays real change).
Conclusion: Summarize the discussion and reaffirm your stance.

env043.title: Impact of Light Pollution
env043.topic: Excessive artificial light at night (light pollution) is affecting both human health and wildlife.
env043.instruction: What are the problems caused by light pollution, and what solutions can be implemented?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce light pollution as a growing environmental concern.
Body 1: Analyze the problems (e.g., disruption of circadian rhythms in humans, confusing migratory birds and nocturnal animals, energy waste).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., using shielded lighting, motion-sensor streetlights, establishing 'dark sky' reserves).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions, emphasizing the importance of natural darkness.

env044.title: Public Transport Fees
env044.topic: The best way to encourage people to use public transport is to make it free of charge.
env044.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your answer with reasons and examples.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on making public transport free to reduce environmental impact.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits (e.g., significant increase in ridership, reducing traffic congestion and emissions, social equity).
Body 2: Discuss other factors that might be more important (e.g., reliability, frequency, comfort, and safety).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps suggesting that quality is just as important as cost.

env045.title: Protecting Endangered Species vs Domestic Agriculture
env045.topic: Some believe that agricultural land should be expanded to feed people, even if it destroys the habitats of endangered species.
env045.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the conflict between food security and habitat preservation.
Body 1: Discuss the case for agricultural expansion (e.g., rising global population, preventing famine, economic development for rural areas).
Body 2: Discuss the case for habitat protection (e.g., importance of biodiversity, preventing irreversible extinction, protecting ecosystem services).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., we should focus on increasing yield on existing land rather than expanding into wild areas).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude on the need for sustainable intensification.

env046.title: Desertification
env046.topic: Desertification is turning once-productive land into barren desert in many regions.
env046.instruction: What are the causes of desertification, and how can it be reversed or slowed down?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Define desertification and its global threat.
Body 1: Discuss the causes (e.g., overgrazing, deforestation, climate change/droughts, poor irrigation practices).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., sustainable land management, reforestation/afforestation projects, 'Great Green Wall' initiatives).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions, stressing the importance of fertile land for future survival.

env047.title: Eco-labeling of Products
env047.topic: All products should be required to have an eco-label showing their environmental impact.
env047.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on mandatory eco-labeling.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits (e.g., empowering consumers to make green choices, forcing companies to be transparent and improve their processes).
Body 2: Discuss the challenges (e.g., complex measuring standards, costs for small businesses, risk of 'greenwashing').
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, advocating for standardized and regulated labels.

env048.title: Ocean Exploration vs Space Exploration
env048.topic: Some people think exploring our oceans is more important than exploring space.
env048.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate between exploring the 'final frontier' of space and our own 'deep blue' oceans.
Body 1: Discuss the importance of ocean exploration (e.g., discovering new medicines, understanding climate impacts, finding sustainable food sources).
Body 2: Discuss the value of space exploration (e.g., technological spin-offs, long-term survival of the species, monitoring Earth from above).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., the ocean is more urgent given its direct link to our climate and survival).
Conclusion: Summarize and finalize your position.

env049.title: Thawing Permafrost
env049.topic: The thawing of permafrost in Arctic regions is releasing vast amounts of methane into the atmosphere.
env049.instruction: What are the consequences of this trend, and how can the global community respond?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Explain the phenomenon of thawing permafrost and its link to global warming.
Body 1: Discuss the consequences (e.g., accelerated warming due to methane release, infrastructure damage in Arctic cities, releasing ancient viruses/bacteria).
Body 2: Propose global responses (e.g., strictly adhering to the Paris Agreement to limit warming, protecting peatlands, investing in methane-capture research).
Conclusion: Summarize and emphasize the dangerous feedback loop of this issue.

env050.title: Technological Optimism
env050.topic: Technology will eventually solve all our environmental problems.
env050.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your level of agreement with the idea that tech is a 'silver bullet' for the environment.
Body 1: Discuss how technology is helping (e.g., renewables, carbon capture, lab-grown meat).
Body 2: Discuss why technology alone is not enough (e.g., requires political will, behavioral changes, and tackling the root cause of overconsumption).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, likely that tech is a tool, not a complete solution on its own.

hea001.title: Responsibility for Healthy Living
hea001.topic: Individuals are responsible for their own health, not the government.
hea001.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the topic and state your position (e.g., partially agree).
Body 1: Discuss the role of individual responsibility (e.g., diet choices, exercise habits, avoiding harmful substances).
Body 2: Discuss the role of the government (e.g., regulating food safety, taxing sugar/tobacco, providing healthcare infrastructure, public health campaigns).
Conclusion: Reiterate that health is a shared responsibility between the individual and the state.

hea002.title: Rise of Childhood Obesity
hea002.topic: Childhood obesity is reaching record levels in many developed and developing countries.
hea002.instruction: What are the causes of this problem, and what solutions can be implemented by schools and parents?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Highlight the alarming trend of childhood obesity.
Body 1: Analyze the causes (e.g., sedentary lifestyle due to technology, consumption of fast food/sugary drinks, lack of physical activity in schools).
Body 2: Propose solutions for parents (e.g., cooking healthy meals, limiting screen time) and schools (e.g., mandatory PE classes, banning junk food in canteens).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions, emphasizing early intervention.

hea003.title: Fast Food Tax
hea003.topic: Some people believe that the government should impose a high tax on fast food to improve public health, while others think this is an unfair burden on low-income families.
hea003.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the debate over taxing unhealthy foods.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of a fast food tax (e.g., discouraging unhealthy eating, generating revenue for healthcare).
Body 2: Discuss the drawbacks (e.g., financial impact on the poor who rely on cheap calories, limiting personal freedom).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., a tax is useful but must be paired with subsidies for healthy food).
Conclusion: Summarize the discussion and reaffirm your stance.

hea004.title: Traditional vs Modern Medicine
hea004.topic: Traditional medicine is more effective than modern medical treatments for certain conditions.
hea004.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your level of agreement with using traditional medicine.
Body 1: Discuss the strengths of modern medicine (e.g., surgery, emergency care, advanced diagnostics).
Body 2: Discuss the value of traditional medicine (e.g., holistic approach, herbal remedies for chronic minor ailments, cultural significance).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps suggesting an integrated approach.

hea005.title: Mental Health in the Workplace
hea005.topic: Stress-related illnesses are becoming more common among employees in high-pressure industries.
hea005.instruction: What are the causes of workplace stress, and what can companies do to support their employees’ mental health?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the growing issue of mental health at work.
Body 1: Discuss the causes (e.g., long hours, job insecurity, lack of work-life balance, high competition).
Body 2: Propose solutions for companies (e.g., mental health days, counseling services, flexible working hours, supportive management culture).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions, emphasizing that a healthy workforce is a productive one.

hea006.title: Private vs Public Healthcare
hea006.topic: Some people believe that healthcare should be entirely free and funded by the government, while others think private healthcare is more efficient.
hea006.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the two models of healthcare systems.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of public healthcare (e.g., accessibility for all, equality, social welfare).
Body 2: Discuss the benefits of private healthcare (e.g., shorter waiting times, better facilities, competition driving innovation).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., a hybrid system is often most practical).
Conclusion: Summarize and finalize your position.

hea007.title: Impact of Advertising on Unhealthy Habits
hea007.topic: The advertising of unhealthy food and drinks should be banned, especially when targeted at children.
hea007.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your answer with reasons.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on banning junk food advertisements.
Body 1: Discuss the negative impact of such ads (e.g., creating lifelong unhealthy habits, misleading young audiences).
Body 2: Discuss why a ban is necessary (e.g., protecting public health, reducing future medical costs for the state).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, supporting stricter regulations or a total ban.

hea008.title: Sedentary Lifestyles
hea008.topic: Modern life has become more sedentary, leading to various health problems like heart disease and diabetes.
hea008.instruction: What are the causes of this sedentary lifestyle, and how can people be encouraged to be more active?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Describe the trend of desk-bound lifestyles in the modern era.
Body 1: Discuss the causes (e.g., desk jobs, entertainment being primarily screen-based, urban design prioritizing cars).
Body 2: Suggest solutions to encourage activity (e.g., workplace exercise breaks, improving cycling/walking infrastructure, fitness apps/wearable tech).
Conclusion: Summarize and emphasize the need for systemic and personal changes.

hea009.title: Organic Food: Health Essential vs Marketing Hype
hea009.topic: Some people believe that eating organic food is essential for good health, while others think it is just a marketing trend.
hea009.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the debate over organic vs. conventional food.
Body 1: Discuss the perceived health benefits of organic food (e.g., no pesticides, higher nutrient levels, better for the environment).
Body 2: Discuss the view that it's marketing hype (e.g., high cost for marginal health gains, conventional food is already safe).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., healthy eating is about variety and whole foods, regardless of 'organic' labels).
Conclusion: Summarize the discussion and provide a final thought.

hea010.title: Free Sports Facilities
hea010.topic: To improve public health, the government should provide free access to sports facilities like gyms and swimming pools.
hea010.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your viewpoint.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on government-funded sports facilities.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits (e.g., removes financial barriers, encourages regular exercise, long-term savings on healthcare).
Body 2: Discuss the challenges (e.g., high cost for taxpayers, maintenance issues, potential for crowding).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps suggesting subsidized rather than entirely free facilities.

hea011.title: Mental Health Awareness vs Physical Health
hea011.topic: Some people believe that societies should prioritize mental health awareness, while others argue that physical health is still the most important factor.
hea011.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the growing debate between prioritizing mental and physical health and state your opinion.
Body 1: Discuss the importance of mental health awareness (e.g., rising rates of depression/anxiety, its impact on productivity, the stigma involved).
Body 2: Discuss the enduring importance of physical health (e.g., prevention of chronic diseases, foundational for all human activities).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., the two are inseparable; a healthy mind requires a healthy body and vice versa).
Conclusion: Summarize both perspectives and reiterate your stance.

hea012.title: Air Pollution and Respiratory Diseases
hea012.topic: Increasing levels of air pollution in cities are leading to a rise in respiratory diseases.
hea012.instruction: What are the effects of this problem on public health, and what can governments do to address it?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Link urban air quality directly to the rise in lung-related health issues.
Body 1: Discuss the effects (e.g., asthma in children, chronic bronchitis, increased hospital admissions, long-term impact on life expectancy).
Body 2: Propose government solutions (e.g., stricter emission standards for vehicles/factories, expanding urban forests, subsidizing clean energy).
Conclusion: Summarize the health impacts and the urgency of environmental policy.

hea013.title: Mandatory Health Checks
hea013.topic: Governments should make regular health check-ups mandatory for all citizens.
hea013.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state your position on the mandatory nature of health screenings.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits (e.g., early detection of life-threatening diseases, reducing long-term healthcare costs, improving national health statistics).
Body 2: Discuss the drawbacks (e.g., invasion of personal privacy, financial burden on the state, potential for 'over-diagnosis').
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps advocating for incentives rather than strict mandates.

hea014.title: Healthcare Technology vs Human Care
hea014.topic: Some people believe that technology can replace many tasks done by doctors and nurses, while others think the 'human touch' is irreplaceable.
hea014.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the rise of AI and robotics in healthcare vs. traditional caregiving.
Body 1: Discuss why technology can replace certain tasks (e.g., robotic surgery, AI diagnostics, automated monitoring of vital signs).
Body 2: Discuss the importance of the human element (e.g., empathy, moral support, complex ethical decision-making, patient trust).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., technology should be a tool to free up medical staff to focus on the human side of care).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude on the future of healthcare.

hea015.title: Sleep Deprivation in Modern Society
hea015.topic: A large percentage of the population suffers from chronic sleep deprivation.
hea015.instruction: What are the causes of this issue, and how does it affect our physical and mental health?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State the prevalence of sleep issues in the digital and high-pressure era.
Body 1: Analyze the causes (e.g., blue light from screens, workplace stress, caffeinated lifestyles, long commuting times).
Body 2: Discuss the health consequences (e.g., weakened immune system, heart problems, cognitive decline, mood disorders).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and effects, emphasizing the need for 'sleep hygiene'.

hea016.title: Sugar Tax on Beverages
hea016.topic: A high tax on sugary drinks is the most effective way to reduce obesity rates.
hea016.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state your level of agreement with the 'sugar tax'.
Body 1: Arguments for the tax (e.g., reducing consumption through price, successful examples in other countries, revenue for health programs).
Body 2: Arguments against or alternative methods (e.g., education is better, industry reformulation of products, tax affecting the poorest most).
Conclusion: Restate your thesis, likely supporting the tax as part of a larger strategy.

hea017.title: Global Pandemic Preparedness
hea017.topic: Some argue that countries should invest more in pandemic prevention, while others think resources should be focused on existing chronic diseases like cancer.
hea017.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the conflict in healthcare funding priorities.
Body 1: Discuss the case for pandemic preparedness (e.g., global economic collapse, sudden mass casualties, international security).
Body 2: Discuss the case for focusing on chronic diseases (e.g., millions of deaths annually, long-term suffering, aging population).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., strengthening general public health infrastructure serves both purposes).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude on a balanced funding approach.

hea018.title: Antibiotic Resistance
hea018.topic: The effectiveness of antibiotics is decreasing due to over-prescription and misuse.
hea018.instruction: What problems does antibiotic resistance cause, and how can the medical community solve this?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the 'silent pandemic' of antibiotic resistance.
Body 1: Discuss the problems (e.g., minor infections becoming fatal, surgery becoming high-risk, increased healthcare costs).
Body 2: Suggest solutions (e.g., stricter regulation of prescriptions, public education on finishing doses, investing in new drug research, reducing antibiotic use in farming).
Conclusion: Summarize the threat and the need for global medical cooperation.

hea019.title: Vaping vs Traditional Smoking
hea019.topic: Vaping and e-cigarettes are just as harmful as traditional tobacco smoking.
hea019.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on the comparative harm of vaping.
Body 1: Discuss the harms of vaping (e.g., nicotine addiction in youth, unknown long-term chemical effects, lung injury).
Body 2: Discuss its role as a harm-reduction tool (e.g., helping adults quit smoking, absence of tar/carbon monoxide).
Conclusion: Summarize and restate your thesis (e.g., it is less harmful for quitters but still dangerous for non-smokers).

hea020.title: Health Benefits of a Vegan Diet
hea020.topic: Some people believe that a vegan diet is the healthiest way to live, while others argue that it lacks essential nutrients.
hea020.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the popular rise of veganism and the nutritional concerns associated with it.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits (e.g., lower risk of heart disease/diabetes, high fiber/antioxidants, weight management).
Body 2: Discuss the risks/deficiencies (e.g., lack of B12, iron, calcium, and protein if not carefully planned).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., any diet can be healthy or unhealthy depending on planning, but a balanced whole-food approach is key).
Conclusion: Summarize both views and provide a closing perspective.

hea021.title: Work-Life Balance and Health
hea021.topic: The lack of work-life balance is the primary cause of poor health in modern society.
hea021.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on how work-life imbalance affects public health.
Body 1: Discuss the impact of overworking (e.g., chronic stress, lack of time for exercise, poor eating habits).
Body 2: Discuss other significant factors (e.g., environmental pollution, genetic factors, availability of healthcare).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, emphasizing that work-life balance is a major, but not the only, factor.

hea022.title: Social Media and Mental Health in Teens
hea022.topic: The use of social media is linked to increasing rates of anxiety and depression among teenagers.
hea022.instruction: What problems does this cause, and what can be done to protect the mental health of young people?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Link social media usage to the current teen mental health crisis.
Body 1: Analyze the problems (e.g., cyberbullying, body image issues due to comparison, sleep disruption).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., parental controls/guidelines, school-based digital literacy programs, social media platforms implementing stricter safety features).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions, stressing the need for a healthier digital environment.

hea023.title: Alternative Medicine vs Science-based Medicine
hea023.topic: Some people believe that alternative medicines (like acupuncture or homeopathy) are effective, while others think they are pseudoscientific and dangerous.
hea023.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the divide between alternative and conventional medicine.
Body 1: Discuss the arguments for alternative medicine (e.g., fewer side effects, holistic approach, psychological benefits).
Body 2: Discuss the concerns about alternative medicine (e.g., lack of scientific evidence, delaying necessary conventional treatment).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., it can be used as a 'complementary' tool but should not replace evidence-based medicine).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude.

hea024.title: Compulsory Physical Education in Universities
hea024.topic: Physical education should be a mandatory subject for all university students, regardless of their major.
hea024.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on mandatory PE at the tertiary level.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits (e.g., combating the sedentary nature of studying, stress relief, long-term health habits).
Body 2: Discuss the drawbacks (e.g., taking time away from academic research, lack of interest, students' right to choose).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps suggesting that it should be encouraged but not compulsory.

hea025.title: Shortage of Medical Professionals in Rural Areas
hea025.topic: In many countries, there is a significant shortage of doctors and nurses in rural areas.
hea025.instruction: What are the causes of this problem, and what measures can be taken to attract medical staff to these locations?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the inequality of healthcare access between urban and rural zones.
Body 1: Analyze the causes (e.g., lack of advanced medical facilities, fewer career advancement opportunities, lower quality of life for families in rural areas).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., government subsidies/higher salaries for rural practitioners, student loan forgiveness for those serving in rural areas, developing telemedicine).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions.

hea026.title: Ban on Artificial Trans Fats
hea026.topic: The use of artificial trans fats in all food products should be legally banned.
hea026.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your level of agreement with a legal ban on trans fats.
Body 1: Discuss the health reasons for a ban (e.g., direct link to heart disease, excessive burden on the public health system).
Body 2: Discuss the industrial perspective (e.g., cost of changing recipes, potential impact on food shelf-life).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, emphasizing that public health outweighs industrial convenience.

hea027.title: State Funding for Organ Transplantation
hea027.topic: Some believe the government should pay for all organ transplant costs, while others think patients should pay for their own surgeries.
hea027.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the financial and ethical dilemma of organ transplant funding.
Body 1: Discuss why the government should pay (e.g., the right to life should not depend on wealth, transplants are life-saving).
Body 2: Discuss why individuals should pay (e.g., high cost for the state, limited budget that could be used for primary prevention).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., government should cover the surgery but strictly regulate the transplant list).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude.

hea028.title: Aging Population and Healthcare Pressure
hea028.topic: The average age of the world's population is increasing, putting immense pressure on healthcare systems.
hea028.instruction: What problems does this cause, and how can societies adapt to an aging population?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Describe the global demographic shift toward an older population.
Body 1: Analyze the problems (e.g., increased demand for geriatric care, rising costs for chronic disease management, smaller workforce to pay for the system).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., investing in home-care technology, extending the retirement age, training more specialized geriatric nurses).
Conclusion: Summarize the challenges and the need for proactive adaptation.

hea029.title: Focusing on Prevention vs Cure
hea029.topic: Governments should spend more money on disease prevention rather than on medical research into cures.
hea029.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state your position on the prevention vs. cure debate.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of prevention (e.g., cheaper in the long run, better quality of life, reducing the need for hospitals).
Body 2: Discuss the necessity of cure research (e.g., many diseases are genetic or unavoidable, need for progress in treating existing patients).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, likely advocating for a balance but prioritizing prevention for public health stability.

hea030.title: Patient Confidentiality vs Public Safety
hea030.topic: Some people believe that doctor-patient confidentiality should never be broken, while others argue it should be breached if the public is at risk.
hea030.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the ethical tension between private medical records and public interest.
Body 1: Discuss the importance of confidentiality (e.g., building patient trust, encouraging people to seek help for sensitive issues).
Body 2: Discuss why public safety might come first (e.g., contagious diseases, risk of violence to others).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., confidentiality is vital but should have clearly defined legal exceptions for immediate danger).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude on the ethical middle ground.

hea031.title: Vegetarianism for All
hea031.topic: The world would be a much healthier place if everyone became a vegetarian.
hea031.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on the health impact of a global shift to vegetarianism.
Body 1: Discuss the health benefits (e.g., lower risk of obesity, heart disease, and high blood pressure).
Body 2: Address the challenges (e.g., risk of nutritional deficiencies like B12/Iron, importance of a balanced diet regardless of meat consumption).
Conclusion: Summarize and restate your final opinion.

hea032.title: Rise in Chronic Diseases
hea032.topic: Despite medical advances, chronic diseases like diabetes and heart disease are increasing worldwide.
hea032.instruction: What are the causes of this trend, and what can be done to reverse it?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Note the paradox between medical progress and the rise of lifestyle diseases.
Body 1: Discuss the causes (e.g., processed food diets, sedentary work life, environmental stressors).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., government regulations on food industry, public health education, designing active cities).
Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions.

hea033.title: Universal Basic Healthcare
hea033.topic: Some people argue that healthcare is a basic human right that should be free, while others believe that the high cost of medical care makes this impossible for many countries.
hea033.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the debate over healthcare as a right versus an economic challenge.
Body 1: Discuss the case for free healthcare (e.g., social equity, preventing preventable deaths, healthier workforce).
Body 2: Discuss the economic barriers (e.g., strain on national budgets, taxes, the cost of advanced medical technology).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., prioritizing primary care for all while allowing private options for specialized care).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude.

hea034.title: Impact of Fast Food Chains
hea034.topic: The growth of international fast-food chains is the main cause of health problems in many countries.
hea034.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on whether fast-food chains are the primary health threat.

Body 2: Discuss other factors (e.g., lack of exercise, stress, rising food costs for healthy alternatives).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps arguing for a shared blame.

hea035.title: Addiction to Digital Entertainment
hea035.topic: Excessive gaming and smartphone use are being recognized as behavioral addictions that harm mental health.

Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., digital detox programs, psychological counseling, education on balanced tech use).
Conclusion: Summarize the impact and emphasize the need for healthy boundaries.

hea036.title: Government Incentives for Healthy Employees
hea036.topic: Some believe that companies should be rewarded by the government if their employees are healthy and fit, while others disagree.
hea036.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the idea of corporate wellness incentives.

Body 2: Discuss the potential for discrimination (e.g., penalizing those with disabilities or genetic conditions, invasion of privacy).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., support incentives for wellness programs, but not for individuals' actual health status).
Conclusion: Summarize and conclude.

hea037.title: Free Vaccinations for All
hea037.topic: The government should provide free vaccinations for every citizen to prevent the spread of diseases.
hea037.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your viewpoint.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on government-funded universal vaccination.

Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, likely supporting free access for safety.

hea038.title: Over-the-Counter Medication Misuse
hea038.topic: The easy availability of over-the-counter (OTC) medication leads to widespread self-medication and misuse.

Conclusion: Summarize the causes and solutions.

hea039.title: Nutritional Education in Schools
hea039.topic: Some people believe that schools should teach nutrition and healthy cooking, while others argue that this is the parents' responsibility.

hea040.title: Impact of Noise Pollution on Health

hea041.title: Universal Healthcare vs Individual Insurance

hea042.title: Impact of Loneliness on Health

hea043.title: Legalizing Euthanasia
hea043.topic: Euthanasia (mercy killing) should be legalized for patients suffering from incurable and painful diseases.

hea044.title: Natural Birth vs Medical Intervention

hea047.title: Mandatory Calorie Labeling in Restaurants

hea050.title: Investing in Cures for Rare Diseases
hea050.topic: Some people believe that medical research should focus on diseases that affect millions, while others argue that rare diseases also deserve equal funding.

Conclusion: Summarize and conclude on an ethical research agenda.

car001.title: Job Satisfaction vs High Salary

Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps suggesting a balance depending on life stages.

car002.title: Staying in One Company vs Job Hopping
car002.topic: Some people argue that it is better to stay in one company for a long period, while others believe that changing jobs frequently is more beneficial for professional growth.
car002.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

car003.title: The Skills Gap in the Labor Market
car003.topic: In many countries, there is a growing gap between the skills graduates possess and the requirements of the modern labor market.
car003.instruction: What are the causes of this 'skills gap,' and what measures can be taken by universities and employers to address this issue?

Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., universities partnering with tech firms, mandatory internships, continuous workplace learning).

car004.title: The Obsolescence of Physical Offices
car004.topic: The rise of remote work has made physical office spaces obsolete and unnecessary for modern businesses.
car004.instruction: Do you agree or disagree with this view? Support your opinion with relevant examples.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Paraphrase the prompt and state your position.

Body 2: Reasons why offices are still necessary (e.g., team culture, spontaneous creativity, separating work and home).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, likely supporting a hybrid model.

car005.title: University Degree vs Vocational Training
car005.topic: Some believe that a university degree is the only way to guarantee a successful career, while others think that vocational training or work experience is more valuable in today’s economy.
car005.instruction: Discuss both views and give your opinion.

Body 3: Give your opinion (e.g., depends on the field; blue-collar vs. white-collar).
Conclusion: Summarize the discussion.

car006.title: The Rise of the Gig Economy

suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the gig economy and its prevalence.

Body 2: Discuss effects (e.g., lack of social security for workers, pressure on traditional companies to adapt, diversified income streams).
Conclusion: Summarize the shift in how society views 'employment'.

car007.title: Foreign Language Proficiency for Success

car009.title: Employee Burnout

car010.title: Universal Basic Income (UBI)

Body 2: Propose government solutions (e.g., subsidizing first-time buyers, implementing rent controls, increasing social housing construction).

car012.title: Gender Equality in Leadership

car013.title: Self-Employment vs Working for an Employer

car016.title: Age Discrimination in the Workplace

car017.title: Workplace Dress Codes: Uniforms vs Casual Wear

car020.title: International Aid vs Domestic Issues
