car023.title: Increasing Retirement Age
car023.topic: Some argue that retirement age should be increased to support the aging population, while others think people should be allowed to retire earlier.
car023.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the debate on retirement age in the context of an aging society.
Body 1: Discuss the benefits of increasing retirement age (e.g., economic stability, utilizing experienced talent, reducing pension strain).
Body 2: Discuss the case for earlier retirement (e.g., personal freedom, opening jobs for youth, physical health limits).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., flexible retirement options based on the nature of the job).
Conclusion: Summarize the main points.

car024.title: Soft Skills vs Technical Skills
car024.topic: Soft skills, such as communication and teamwork, are more valuable than technical skills in the modern workplace.
car024.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on the hierarchy of skills in today's job market.
Body 1: Discuss the importance of soft skills (e.g., adaptability, leadership, conflict resolution in complex teams).
Body 2: Acknowledge the role of technical skills (e.g., foundation for task execution, specific expertise that AI cannot yet fully replace).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, likely advocating for a balance but highlighting the durability of soft skills.

car025.title: Child Labor in Global Supply Chains
car025.topic: The use of child labor is still a serious issue in the global supply chains of some industries.
car025.instruction: What are the causes of this problem, and how can it be eliminated?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the unethical persistence of child labor in modern manufacturing.
Body 1: Analyze causes (e.g., extreme poverty, lack of educational access, corporate greed/lack of oversight).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., strict international trade laws, mandatory supply chain audits, corporate boycotts, supporting local education).
Conclusion: Emphasize the shared responsibility of governments, companies, and consumers.

car026.title: Hard Work vs Luck and Connections
car026.topic: Some believe that success in business depends on hard work, while others think luck and connections are more important.
car026.instruction: Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the two perspectives on the secret to business success.
Body 1: Discuss the role of hard work (e.g., persistence, skill mastery, building a solid foundation).
Body 2: Discuss the role of luck and connections (e.g., being in the right place at the right time, access to capital/mentorship through networks).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., hard work prepares you to take advantage of luck).
Conclusion: Summarize the discussion.

car027.title: Mandatory Work from Home Days
car027.topic: Employees should be allowed to work from home at least two days a week.
car027.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your viewpoint.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on the implementation of a hybrid work model.
Body 1: Benefits of working from home (e.g., improved work-life balance, reduced commuting time/costs, environmental impact).
Body 2: Potential drawbacks (e.g., communication silos, isolation, difficulty for junior staff to learn).
Conclusion: Restate your stance, likely supporting flexibility.

car028.title: Urban Migration and Rural Economies
car028.topic: More people are choosing to move from rural areas to big cities for work opportunities.
car028.instruction: What are the causes of this urban migration, and what are its effects on rural economies?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the global trend of urbanization.
Body 1: Causes (e.g., concentration of wealth/jobs in cities, better infrastructure, educational opportunities).
Body 2: Effects on rural economies (e.g., aging population in villages, loss of agricultural labor, reduced local services/investment).
Conclusion: Highlight the need for balanced regional development.

car029.title: Local Businesses vs E-commerce Giants
car029.topic: Small local businesses are struggling to compete with massive global e-commerce platforms.
car029.instruction: How can small businesses survive, and what role should the government play?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State the threat posed by giants like Amazon to local shops.
Body 1: Strategies for small businesses (e.g., focusing on niche markets, personalized customer service, omnichannel approach).
Body 2: Government's role (e.g., fair taxation for tech giants, grants for digitalizing small businesses, promoting local shopping).
Conclusion: Emphasize that small businesses are the backbone of communities.

car030.title: Fixed Salary vs Commission-Based Income
car030.topic: Some people prefer a fixed salary, while others prefer a commission-based income where they are paid based on their performance.
car030.instruction: Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the two payment models and state your opinion.
Body 1: Benefits of a fixed salary (e.g., financial predictability, less stress, easier budgeting).
Body 2: Benefits of commission-based income (e.g., high incentive for top performers, meritocracy, potential for higher earnings).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., a base salary plus commission is often the best compromise).
Conclusion: Summarize the main points.

car031.title: Banning Overtime Work
car031.topic: Overtime work should be banned to protect the mental and physical health of employees.
car031.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on the proposal to outlaw overtime.
Body 1: Discuss the health benefits (e.g., reducing burnout, improving sleep, better work-life balance).
Body 2: Discuss the economic/practical drawbacks (e.g., reduced income for workers, difficulty for businesses to meet urgent deadlines).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, perhaps suggesting strict regulation instead of a total ban.

car032.title: Restarting Careers After a Long Break
car032.topic: Many people find it difficult to restart their careers after a long break (e.g., for childcare or health reasons).
car032.instruction: What challenges do they face, and how can employers support them?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the problem of career gaps and the 'returnship' challenge.
Body 1: Analyze challenges (e.g., outdated skills, loss of professional network, employer bias against gaps).
Body 2: Propose solutions for employers (e.g., retraining programs, flexible onboarding, focus on transferable skills).
Conclusion: Emphasize the benefit of a diverse and experienced workforce.

car033.title: Regulating Executive Salaries
car033.topic: Some believe that the government should regulate the maximum salary of top executives, while others think companies should be free to pay whatever they want.
car033.instruction: Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the debate over executive compensation caps.
Body 1: Discuss the case for regulation (e.g., reducing wealth inequality, preventing corporate greed, social justice).
Body 2: Discuss the case for free market pay (e.g., attracting global talent, rewarding high-stakes performance, government overreach).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., transparency and tax-based solutions are better than hard caps).
Conclusion: Summarize the main points.

car034.title: Encouraging Entrepreneurship in Youth
car034.topic: Young people should be encouraged to start their own businesses rather than looking for jobs in existing companies.
car034.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Provide reasons for your answer.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on youth entrepreneurship vs. traditional employment.
Body 1: Benefits of starting a business (e.g., innovation, job creation, personal independence).
Body 2: Risks and the value of traditional jobs (e.g., high failure rate, lack of initial experience, value of learning from established mentors).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, likely advocating for a balance based on individual passion.

car035.title: Rapid Consumerism in Developing Nations
car035.topic: Consumerism is increasing rapidly in developing countries.
car035.instruction: What are the causes of this trend, and what are the effects on the national economy?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Define the rise of consumerist culture in emerging markets.
Body 1: Analyze causes (e.g., rising middle class, globalization of brands, easy access to credit).
Body 2: Discuss effects (e.g., economic growth via demand, but also increased household debt and environmental waste).
Conclusion: Summarize the economic trade-offs.

car036.title: Work Stress and Productivity
car036.topic: Work-related stress is leading to a decrease in productivity in many organizations.
car036.instruction: What can employers do to reduce stress and increase efficiency?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Link workplace stress to economic inefficiency.
Body 1: Identify triggers (e.g., unrealistic deadlines, toxic management, lack of recognition).
Body 2: Propose solutions for employers (e.g., wellness programs, transparent communication, manageable workloads).
Conclusion: Emphasize that happy employees are more productive.

car037.title: Experience vs Personality in Hiring
car037.topic: Some people think that a person's suitability for a job should be based on their experience, while others think personality is more important.
car037.instruction: Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the two criteria for hiring and state your opinion.
Body 1: Discuss the importance of experience (e.g., technical competence, immediate productivity, reduced training costs).
Body 2: Discuss the importance of personality (e.g., cultural fit, coachability, teamwork, emotional intelligence).
Body 3: Give your opinion (e.g., personality is often harder to change than skills).
Conclusion: Summarize the discussion.

car038.title: Tourism and Economic Growth
car038.topic: Tourism is the most effective way for a country to grow its economy.
car038.instruction: Do you agree or disagree? Support your answer with relevant examples.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on tourism as a primary economic engine.
Body 1: Benefits of tourism (e.g., foreign exchange, job creation in services, infrastructure development).
Body 2: Risks/Drawbacks (e.g., seasonal volatility, environmental damage, over-reliance on a single sector).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, likely suggesting economic diversification.

car039.title: Brain Drain in Developing Nations
car039.topic: Brain drain, the migration of highly skilled people to other countries, is a major problem for developing nations.
car039.instruction: What are the causes, and what can be done to encourage people to stay?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Define brain drain and its negative impact on origin countries.
Body 1: Analyze causes (e.g., lack of high-paying jobs, political instability, better education/health abroad).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., improving domestic working conditions, government research grants, repatriating talent through incentives).
Conclusion: Highlight the need for systemic national improvement.

car040.title: Profit vs Social Responsibility
car040.topic: Some people argue that companies should only focus on making a profit, while others believe they have a social responsibility to the community.
car040.instruction: Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the debate over corporate goals and state your opinion.
Body 1: Discuss the focus on profit (e.g., responsibility to shareholders, reinvestment, driving economic efficiency).
Body 2: Discuss social responsibility (e.g., ethical brand image, environmental sustainability, supporting local charities).
Body 3: Give your opinion (e.g., CSR actually leads to long-term profitability).
Conclusion: Summarize the main points.

car041.title: The Four-Day Workweek
car041.topic: The four-day workweek is a better model for modern society than the traditional five-day week.
car041.instruction: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on the shift to a shorter workweek.
Body 1: Discuss benefits (e.g., increased productivity, improved mental health, lower operational costs for offices).
Body 2: Discuss challenges (e.g., potential for longer daily hours, difficulty in customer-facing industries).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, likely supporting it as the future of work.

car042.title: Workplace Monitoring and Privacy
car042.topic: Many employees feel that their jobs are being monitored too closely by technology (e.g., tracking software).
car042.instruction: What problems does this cause, and how can a balance be found?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the rise of 'bossware' and employee surveillance.
Body 1: Analyze problems (e.g., erosion of trust, increased anxiety, stifled creativity).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., transparency in data usage, focusing on output rather than activity, legal privacy protections).
Conclusion: Summarize the need for mutual trust in the digital workplace.

car043.title: Generalist vs Specialist
car043.topic: Some people think it is better to be a 'generalist' (knowing a little about many things), while others think it is better to be a 'specialist' (knowing a lot about one thing).
car043.instruction: Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the two career philosophies and state your opinion.
Body 1: Benefits of being a generalist (e.g., adaptability, broad problem-solving, suitable for leadership).
Body 2: Benefits of being a specialist (e.g., high expertise, high demand in niche fields, higher potential salary).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., the 'T-shaped' professional model).
Conclusion: Summarize the main points.

car044.title: Investing in the Green Economy
car044.topic: Governments should invest more in the green economy (renewable energy) even if it leads to slower economic growth initially.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on prioritizing the environment over short-term GDP growth.
Body 1: Reasons to invest (e.g., long-term sustainability, creating new 'green' jobs, preventing climate-related economic collapse).
Body 2: Acknowledge the economic risks (e.g., high initial costs, inflation, transition pain for fossil fuel workers).
Conclusion: Reiterate that long-term survival is the ultimate economic foundation.

car045.title: Automation in Manufacturing
car045.topic: Automation is being widely adopted in the manufacturing sector.
car045.instruction: What are the reasons for this, and what are the effects on the manual labor market?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the shift toward automated factories.
Body 1: Analyze reasons (e.g., consistency/quality, 24/7 production, long-term cost reduction).
Body 2: Analyze effects (e.g., displacement of manual workers, need for retraining, higher safety levels).
Conclusion: Summarize the industrial transformation.

car046.title: Childcare Barriers to Work
car046.topic: The lack of affordable childcare prevents many parents from returning to the workforce.
car046.instruction: What are the consequences of this, and what solutions can be implemented?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Highlight the link between childcare costs and labor participation.
Body 1: Consequences (e.g., loss of female talent, increased household poverty, lower national productivity).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., government subsidies, workplace nurseries, flexible hours for parents).
Conclusion: Summarize the importance of family-friendly labor policies.

car047.title: Paid vs Unpaid Internships
car047.topic: Some believe that internships should always be paid, while others argue that the experience gained is enough compensation.
car047.instruction: Discuss both views and give your opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the debate over intern compensation.
Body 1: Discuss the case for paid internships (e.g., social mobility for those who can't afford to work for free, ethics of labor).
Body 2: Discuss the arguments for unpaid roles (e.g., low training value for companies initially, focus on learning).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., unpaid internships are exploitative and should be regulated).
Conclusion: Summarize the main points.

car048.title: Changing Careers Several Times
car048.topic: In a modern economy, a person should expect to change their career (not just their job) several times in their life.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: State your position on the necessity of career pivoting in the 21st century.
Body 1: Reasons why it's necessary (e.g., industry disruption, longer life expectancy, personal growth).
Body 2: Challenges of career changing (e.g., financial risk, need for continuous retraining).
Conclusion: Reiterate your stance, advocating for 'lifelong learning'.

car049.title: Widening Economic Inequality
car049.topic: Economic inequality is widening in many developed nations.
car049.instruction: What are the main causes of this gap, and what can be done to ensure a fairer distribution of wealth?
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Introduce the problem of the wealth gap in advanced economies.
Body 1: Analyze causes (e.g., automation displacing middle-class jobs, tax loopholes for the wealthy, rising cost of education).
Body 2: Propose solutions (e.g., progressive taxation, higher minimum wages, universal access to high-quality education).
Conclusion: Summarize the need for a more equitable economic system.

car050.title: Value of Team-Building Activities
car050.topic: Some people think that team-building activities are a waste of time, while others believe they are essential for a healthy work environment.
car050.instruction: Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
suggestedStructure: Introduction: Present the debate over corporate team-building events.
Body 1: Discuss why some see them as a waste (e.g., 'forced fun', taking time away from actual tasks, introverted discomfort).
Body 2: Discuss the benefits (e.g., improved communication, breaking down silos, fostering trust).
Body 3: Give your own opinion (e.g., they work only if they are authentic and voluntary).
Conclusion: Summarize the main points.
